question: Utilize "agency copilot" and WorkIQ to get the name of the last person that you sent an email to.
hint: Sometimes, even if an agent is setup to have MCP servers and connections like WorkIQ, you must specifically name them in your prompt
answer: Using WorkIQ, go to my 'Sent' items in Outlook and fetch only the name of the contact from my last sent email. If there are multiple, return only the first person found.

question: Get the title of the next upcoming event from the Events calendar list on microsoft.sharepoint.com.
hint: You can't scrape the rendered SharePoint page — launch Agency with --mcp sharepoint and have it query the list by its GUID instead.
answer: Using the SharePoint MCP server, find the next upcoming event on the MSW Events list (https://microsoft.sharepoint.com/_layouts/15/Events.aspx?ListGuid=1724fca7-44cb-4d42-9942-5342faa991ff) and tell me its title, date/time, and location. Heads up: the list has lots of items sorted oldest-first, so start from the highest pages and work backward instead of paging up from the beginning.

question: Screen-scrape the title and publish date of the latest post on the Official Microsoft Blog.
hint: No connector and no download button — give the agent the exact URL and tell it which elements to read straight from the rendered page.
answer: Fetch https://blogs.microsoft.com, screen-scrape the most recent blog post, and return its title, author, and publish date by parsing the page HTML (no API or export is available).

question: Get an exact number out of a public dataset — by running code, not eyeballing it.
hint: M365 chat eyeballs a table and hallucinates totals. A CLI agent downloads the real file and computes — so ask for the code and its output.
answer: Download https://raw.githubusercontent.com/microsoft/ai-diffusion-report/main/data/AI_Diffusion_Q12026_Update.csv and write and run a script that prints the exact number of economies above 30% AI diffusion in Q1 2026 and the average Q1 2026 diffusion across all economies. Show the code and its output.

question: Reshape a raw public dataset into a clean, app-ready file.
hint: Chat can describe cleanup; the agent writes the file. Name the exact columns and filename.
answer: Using that CSV, write and run code that converts the percent strings to numbers, adds a change column (Q1 2026 minus H2 2025), ranks economies by Q1 2026, and saves data/ai_diffusion.csv with columns economy,h1_2025,h2_2025,q1_2026,change,rank. Print the top 5 rows.

question: Follow a news post to its source data, then fact-check the article against it.
hint: Give the agent only the blog post. It has to navigate to the public data itself, then verify — and honestly flag any claim the data can't confirm.
answer: Starting from only https://blogs.microsoft.com/on-the-issues/2026/05/07/the-state-of-global-ai-diffusion-in-2026/, follow its links to the public dataset on GitHub and download it. Then write and run code to fact-check the article's numeric claims (economies above 30%, the UAE rate, the US rate) against the data. Output a table: claim, article_value, computed_value, verdict — and flag any claim the dataset can't verify.
